reasoning: The question compares the ECBASSETSW value on 2026-04-05 (which will correspond to the week ending Friday, April 3) with 2026-03-29 (week ending Friday, March 27). The April 3 financial statement will be the first of Q2 2026 and therefore must incorporate the ECB's quarterly revaluation of gold and foreign exchange reserves. Historical data shows that these quarter-end revaluations consistently produce massive positive spikes in total assets that dwarf the slow, steady decline from quantitative tightening (APP and PEPP runoff). Specifically, recent quarter-end reports on Oct 3, 2025, and Jan 2, 2026, showed week-over-week asset jumps of +120 billion and +158 billion euros, respectively. Statistical analysis also notes a positive seasonal effect at this time of year. Unforeseen negative revaluations could occur if gold or FX plunged, but the strong upward skew of these adjustments yields high confidence in an overall increase.
